reason: Infra / MEV cluster linked by [solana, upgrade, rpc, trading, triton]

sampleTweets.text:
Trading is a zero-sum game. You compete on strategy, speed, or both.

And while being the smartest is your challenge, being the first depends on your infrastructure (and it just got a major speed boost).

Deshred transactions, our new streaming endpoint, lets you see Solana transactions before the RPC node executes them. It taps into the validator pipeline at the Blockstore stage and delivers fully reconstructed transactions over gRPC, p50 ~6.3ms ahead of the regular stream.

- In production on Triton
- Open-source (+ merged into Agave 4.0)
- Node.js SDK ready

Before you ask if it's private flow (it isn't), here's how it works under the hood.

On Triton's infrastructure, Deshred delivers transactions at p50 ~6.3ms, p90 ~20ms, and p99 ~79ms ahead of executed transaction delivery.

~20% of Solana transactions land in a block with a failed status. The other 80% carry real intent. HFT, arbitrage, liquidations, and copy trading all come down to who sees the signal first.

With Deshred, that's you.

W for open-source on Solana🌊

Self-hostable multisig UI with verifiable builds, Token-2022 support, vault management, program upgrades, and custom RPC. 

Solid foundation from @multisig

reason: Wallets cluster linked by [wallet, yield, security, ledger]

sampleTweets.text:
the most underrated security feature on your hardware wallet... and yet 90% of people skip it

Passphrase is a secret word you add on top of your 24 word seed phrase. 🔒

> it generates a completely separate wallet. same device, different vault.
> basically a second cold wallet for free
> even if someone finds your seed phrase, they get nothing without the passphrase
> takes 2 minutes to set up. zero extra cost. no excuses > adds 100% extra security on top of your existing cold wallet setup
> if someone forces you to open your ledger, you open the wallet without the passphrase. they see nothing important
> no passphrase stored anywhere on the device. it only exists in your head/physical storage

your 24 words is the lock. the passphrase is the lock behind the lock.

if you have a hardware wallet and no passphrase, you're halfway protected.

i'll be sharing other important security features, follow/support for more! 🫂

What wallet are you using?

you're in crypto and holding $50k+ onchain? 
make sure to: 

> have at least 2 hardware wallets from different manufacturers
> dedicated device for crypto only. no browsing, no apps, no email 
> seed phrases stored in 2 separate physical locations
> cold wallet for holding. hot wallet for interacting. never mix the two
> add a 25th word passphrase to your hardware wallet. seed alone is not enough
> always on VPN on every device you use for crypto
> separate email exclusively for crypto
> no cloud backup. ever. iCloud, Google Drive are not your friends
> hardware wallets stored in a safe. not on your desk
> limit your onchain interactions. every dapp is an exposure point
> never post wallet addresses or portfolio sizes publicly
> every 3 months, review all approvals, connected apps, and wallet activity
> keep your crypto identity and your real identity fully separate

most people with serious size are still operating like they have nothing to lose.

please do it, now! not later.
